message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: My task: add custom Jinja2 filters support to the prompt decorator (filters param, create_jinja_env helper). Only touches outlines/prompts.py. What file(s) does your feature need? If you also need prompts.py, let's coordinate carefully on non-overlapping regions or I can incorporate your changes.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: My feature: global filter registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) in outlines/prompts.py, modifying Prompt._template_from_str/_template_from_file to include global filters. This is the only file my feature touches. What file(s) does your feature need? If it also needs outlines/prompts.py, let's coordinate carefully on non-overlapping regions or sequence edits.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Same file, overlapping regions (_template_from_str/_template_from_file) - unavoidable since both features touch template creation. Let's each implement independently and I'll do the final manual merge/reconciliation (I'm the one running the merge step). Please keep your register_filter/unregister_filter additions as a self-contained global dict + two functions, and only touch _template_from_str/_template_from_file to loop in that global dict when building the env - that'll make combining with my create_jinja_env(filters=...) helper straightforward. Ping me with DONE + a brief summary of your exact function names/signatures when finished so I can merge cleanly.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Our features are complementary but both touch outlines/prompts.py (yours: local 'filters' param on prompt()/Prompt/_template_from_str/_template_from_file; mine: register_filter/unregister_filter global registry + merging into env.filters, with local filters taking precedence). Since it's a single small file, let's each implement independently on our own branches, then merge and hand-resolve any conflicts in the final git merge step (as required by the submission protocol) rather than trying to split the file into disjoint regions. I'll design my change to be additive (module-level _FILTERS dict + two new functions + minimal insertion into env-creation code) so it merges cleanly with your changes. Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Implemented: module-level _FILTERS: Dict[str, Callable] = {} dict; register_filter(name: str, func: Callable) -> None; unregister_filter(name: str) -> None. In _template_from_str and _template_from_file, added 'env.filters.update(_FILTERS)' right after the built-in filters are set (in _template_from_str, that's after env.filters['args']=get_fn_args; in _template_from_file, right before env.get_template(...) since it had no filters before). All existing tests pass (tests/test_prompts.py, 13 passed). Since local filters should take precedence over global per the spec, when you add your local 'filters' param, just apply env.filters.update(filters) AFTER my env.filters.update(_FILTERS) line so locals win. Committing now.

type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Implemented: prompt(fn=None, filters=None) supporting both @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}) usage; Prompt.from_str/from_file now accept filters param; added create_jinja_env(loader, filters) helper used by _template_from_str/_template_from_file (replaces the two inline jinja2.Environment(...) constructions). All existing tests pass. Ready to merge whenever you're done - ping me.

type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Merged team/agent2 into agent1, resolved conflicts by wiring _FILTERS into create_jinja_env (global filters applied first, then local 'filters' param overrides on clash). Verified: global register_filter/unregister_filter works, local filters take precedence over global, all 13 existing tests pass. patch.txt rebuilt from merged tree and pushed. All good on my end.
